root_text.an4.175:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā upavāṇo yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.175:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā upavāṇo āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.175:2.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, vijjāyantakaro hotī”ti?
root_text.an4.175:10.1: “‘Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, vijjāyantakaro hotī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.175:10.3: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, caraṇenantakaro hotī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.175:10.5: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, vijjācaraṇenantakaro hotī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.175:10.7: ‘Kiṁ panāvuso sāriputta, aññatra vijjācaraṇenantakaro hotī’ti, iti puṭṭho samāno:
root_text.an4.175:11.1: “Vijjāya ce, āvuso, antakaro abhavissa, saupādānova samāno antakaro abhavissa.
root_text.an4.175:11.2: Caraṇena ce, āvuso, antakaro abhavissa, saupādānova samāno antakaro abhavissa.
root_text.an4.175:11.3: Vijjācaraṇena ce, āvuso, antakaro abhavissa, saupādānova samāno antakaro abhavissa.
root_text.an4.175:11.4: Aññatra vijjācaraṇena ce, āvuso, antakaro abhavissa, puthujjano antakaro abhavissa.
root_text.an4.175:11.6: Caraṇavipanno kho, āvuso, yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati.

translation_text.an4.175:1.1: Then Venerable Upavāṇa went up to Venerable Sāriputta, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.175:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, Upavāṇa sat down to one side, and said to Sāriputta:
translation_text.an4.175:2.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, do you become a terminator because of knowledge?”
translation_text.an4.175:4.1: “Do you become a terminator because of conduct?”
translation_text.an4.175:6.1: “Do you become a terminator because of both knowledge and conduct?”
translation_text.an4.175:8.1: “Do you become a terminator for some reason other than knowledge and conduct?”
translation_text.an4.175:10.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, when asked whether you become a terminator because of knowledge or conduct or knowledge and conduct, or for some other reason,
translation_text.an4.175:10.9: How then do you become a terminator?”
translation_text.an4.175:11.1: “Reverend, if you became a terminator because of knowledge, then even someone who still grasps fuel could be a terminator.
translation_text.an4.175:11.2: If you became a terminator because of conduct, then even someone who still grasps fuel could be a terminator.
translation_text.an4.175:11.3: If you became a terminator because of both knowledge and conduct, then even someone who still grasps fuel could be a terminator.
translation_text.an4.175:11.4: If you became a terminator for some reason other than knowledge and conduct, then even an ordinary person could be a terminator.
translation_text.an4.175:11.5: For an ordinary person lacks knowledge and conduct.
translation_text.an4.175:11.6: Reverend, someone lacking good conduct does not know and see things as they are.
translation_text.an4.175:11.7: Someone accomplished in good conduct knows and sees things as they are.
translation_text.an4.175:11.8: Knowing and seeing things as they are, one is a terminator.”
